mfa.title: Two-factor authentication
mfa.subtitle: Add a one-time code from an authenticator app to your sign-ins
mfa.disabledDescription: Two-factor authentication is off. Turn it on to better protect your account.
mfa.setupDescription: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app, then enter the code it shows.
mfa.step1: Open your authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator, Aegis, 1Password).
mfa.step2: Scan the QR code, or enter the secret key manually.
mfa.step3: Enter the 6-digit code shown by the app to confirm.
mfa.setupError: Couldn't start two-factor setup. Please try again.
mfa.enableError: That code didn't work. Please try again.
mfa.disableDescription: Confirm your identity to turn off two-factor authentication.
mfa.disableError: Couldn't disable two-factor authentication. Check your details and try again.
mfa.regenerateDescription: This invalidates your previous backup codes. Confirm your identity to continue.
mfa.regenerateError: Couldn't regenerate backup codes. Check your details and try again.

backupCodes.title: Save your backup codes
backupCodes.description: Use a backup code to sign in if you lose access to your authenticator app.
backupCodes.warning: These codes are shown only once. Store them somewhere safe — each code works a single time.
